leanInto.name: Kira
leanInto.engineCoachLine: Sim-optimal for sustained DPS: first spike lands around minute 12 (Viper), third item by minute 25. Take Demiurge: +20.3% on this kit’s main output at minute 15.

plan: Queue midlane as primary. It is your best role at 53.0% over 159 games (your overall is 52.0%). Set carry as secondary, not the other way around.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Kira. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 4.9 more games per 100 on it.
Your champion pool is 16+ heroes wide and your top three are only 30% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
The math: you are at 624 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 922 — you have already touched Platinum-level rating once; this is a consistency problem, not a ceiling problem.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: You win the long game
insights.finding: Past 32 minutes you win 58% vs 20% in short games. Scaling comps and patience suit you; avoid coin-flip early all-ins.
insights.receipt: wins 2 of 10 short games vs 7 of 12 long ones, recent sample

insights.title: Cuban brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Cuban Noobster is 56% over 202 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 3 pentakills in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 19 (plus 2 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Goldilocks324 has receipts.

insights.title: Your lean, relative to the squad: vision work
insights.finding: Goldilocks324's profile sits mid-pack on most axes; the clearest lean is vision work at 1.3x the squad average. Steady is a style too — the lineup can build around predictability.

coachReasoning.assessment: You showed both sides of your game recently — a clean 11/4/8 GRIM.exe carry game and back-to-back winning Iggy & Scorch offlanes (7/4/8, 4/1/7 with just 1 death), but also a 2/8/4 Drongo loss where 8 deaths sank you. Your biggest lever is leaning into your scaling picks and dying less in the lane phase, because you clearly win the long game.
plan: On Kira, play for your minute-12 first-item spike (Viper) and your third item by minute 25 — early on, poke safely and farm to that spike rather than forcing coin-flip all-ins, then take Demiurge for the biggest bump to your main damage at 15.

On Iggy & Scorch, keep doing what won those two offlanes — your low-death 4/1/7 game shows the pattern: zone with your fire trail and turret to control the lane from range instead of over-committing to trades.
On Drongo, the 2/8/4 loss was a death spike — position at the back of fights and use your dash to reset spacing, not to chase, so you stop feeding the 8-death games.
On GRIM.exe, your 11/4/8 shows you can hard-carry — after a lead, group and close on objectives rather than farming out, since your data says you convert the long game.
Trust patience as your win condition: past 32 minutes you win far more often, so on any of your carries draft and play to scale — avoid the early all-in and let your item spikes decide the game.

insights.title: You win the long game
insights.finding: You win 58% past 32 minutes versus 20% in short games — your recent Iggy & Scorch and GRIM.exe wins fit that mold, so scaling picks and patience are your climb, not early coin-flips.

insights.title: Kira is a hidden weapon
insights.finding: 57.7% over 137 games on Kira — about 4.9 more wins per 100 than the average Kira player; her minute-12 spike and Demiurge (+20.3% main output at 15) make her your sharpest pick to spam.

insights.title: Cut the death spikes
insights.finding: Your losses were the games you died most — 8 on Drongo, 5 on the offlane loss — versus a 3-4 death norm; the 1-death 4/1/7 win shows disciplined spacing already lives in your hands.

insights.title: Vision is your quiet strength
insights.finding: Your vision work sits at 1.3x the squad average — that map control pairs perfectly with your scaling style; keep it up to safely reach your late-game spikes.

coachReasoningSource: in-session pred-scout-coach agent, grounded in the report + the player’s recent reviewed games; every number ground-checked, failing lines dropped
